observations.recommendation: We recommended that the non-existing or missing PPE items without available record of accountability be identified and be derecognized from the books of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of government agencies to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), strictly:
a) Mandate the Accountant and GSO to promptly and meticulously post the acquisition of PPE to the respective ledger cards and property cards upon receipt of the source documents and keep and maintain the same in accordance with the provisions of the Manual;
b) Mandate the GSO to retain and keep copies of the AIR, PAR, and ARE as prescribed; and
c) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January 31, of each year.

We recommend that the LCE:
a. Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection and post repair inspection to LGU properties that are subjected to repair and furnish the reports to the COA Auditors within five (5) days from the date of each of the inspection activity;
b. Direct the General Service Officer to maintain the records regarding the history of repairs of each of the properties of the LGU; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims pertaining to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and heavy equipment with lacking supporting documents.

We recommended the management to submit the 20% Development Fund on the structural projects for contract review and inspection, in accordance with Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.1.4 to 3.1.7 of the COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that all accountable officers prepare and submit the RAAF to the Office of the Treasurer at the end of each month to ensure all accountable forms issued are properly recorded and reported. Also, we recommended that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the CRAAF to the Office of the Auditor as required under Section 51 of the NGAS, Volume II, for LGUs.

1.8 Therefore, we recommended that the Management identify and derecognize the
non-existing or missing PPE items without available record of accountability
from the books of accounts, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of
government agencies to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to
existence, condition and accountability.

2.10 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the LCE faithfully:
a) Mandate the GSO to retain and keep copies of the AIR, PAR, and ARE
as prescribed;
b) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed
physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the RPCPPE
to the audit team not later than January 31 of succeeding year; and
c) Mandate the Municipal Accountant to recognize the appropriate
depreciation expenses of the PPE accounts.

3.7 We recommended that the Management submit the 20% Development Fund on
the infrastructure projects for contract review, technical review and inspection,
in accordance with Section 122 of the PD No. 1445, otherwise known as the
Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.1.4 to 3.1.7 of the
COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

4.9 We recommended that the LCE instruct the following personnel:
For the Supply Officer:
a. Abide to Section D.7 of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of
Government Water Vehicle.
b. Prepare the Monthly Fuel Consumption.
c. Submit to the Auditor the Driver’s Trip Tickets and Monthly Fuel
Consumption.
For the Drivers
a. To adhere to Section D.6 of the above Manual.
b. To prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels where he summarizes in
chronological order his trip for the month.
c. To submit the above report to the Chief Accountant.
For the Chief Accountant
a. To submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels of the Drivers to the
Auditor.

5.6 Hence, we recommended that the LCE:
a. Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection and
post repair inspection to LGU properties that are subjected to repair and
furnish the reports to the COA Auditors within five (5) days from the date
of each of the inspection activity;
b. Direct the General Service Officer to maintain the records regarding the
history of repairs of each of the properties of the LGU; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims pertaining to repairs
and maintenance of motor vehicles and heavy equipment with lacking
supporting documents.

6.6 We required the MGSO to immediately furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy
of Acceptance & Inspection Report within 24 hours after acceptance of all
consummable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the
LGU personnel concerned on other items as required by Section 114 (d) & 116
of COA No.92-386 date October 12, 1992 and Sec. 5.01.04 of COA Circular No.
95-006 dated May 18, 1995 so that timely inspection of deliveries can be made
by the audit team.

In order to ensure the timely submission of required report and disbursement vouchers (DVs), we recommended to the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required reports and DVs within the prescribed time pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) should (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to reconcile their books and recalculate the amount of both depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation, (b) reflect and present the Property, Plant and Equipment Account (PPE) in the FS with correct amounts, and (c) instruct the General Services Officer to submit to the audit team the Report of the Physical Count on the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), pursuant to Section 262 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units, Volume I and Section 124 (2) of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting System (PPSAS) 17-Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, the management told us the following: (a) The Municipal Mayor has told us that they already hired a Municipal GSO in their plantilla to address all the concerns in all findings concerning the office; (b) They are committed to finish their inventory and reconciliation of their PPE and submit their RCPPE but due to pandemic, it has been set aside; and (c)The Municipal Accountant committed to submit RCPPE within the year of 2021.
observations.reason: There were no designated GSO to focus on the said recommendation.

observations.recommendation: As required by COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015, we recommended the Honorable Mayor to: a. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the physical count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon; b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the Local Road Network account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said account in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with Item VI.2 of COA 2015-008; c. Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every accounting period as well as keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and d. Require Municipal Engineer to provide the municipal accountant and the GSO with complete description and cost of segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, the management explained that they have a newly hired Municipal Engineer as the previous one resigned during the year of CY 2020. They committed to comply with the said findings.
observations.reason: For FY 2020, we also noted that until now, the Inventory Committee was not yet created despite the repeatedly reiterated audit findings for past year, reminding the management to create such committee and its significant role in property management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to: a. Implement the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases and movements of the supplies, in accordance with Sections 182 to 190, Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Volume I; b. The management shall recognize Supplies Inventory at its book first and follow the moving average method for costing in accordance with IPSAS 12, par 35 and 44 and sections 180 to 181, respectively; and c. Conduct physical count at the year-end for faithful compliance with Section 194 of GAM for LGUs, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has told us that they already hired a Municipal GSO in their plantilla to address all the concerns in all findings concerning the office; and The Municipal Accountant has signified commitment on complying this findings through the instruction of the Municipal Mayor.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is overloaded and has no staff support to help her. This has not been the focus of the management.

observations.recommendation: We required the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to immediately furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of Acceptance & Inspection Report within 24 hours after acceptance of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the LGU personnel concerned on other items as required by Section 114 (d) & 116 of COA No.92-386 date October 12, 1992 and Sec. 5.01.04 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 so that timely inspection of deliveries can be made by the audit team.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, it was disclosed that they are not aware with the said circulars. Through our AOM, they signified that they will comply with the said requirements of by COA Circular No.92-386 and COA Circular No. 95-006.
observations.reason: As of June 21, 2022, no submission has been made.

observations.recommendation:
5.7 Therefore, we recommended that the non-existing or missing PPE
items without available record of accountability be identified and
derecognized from the books of accounts, in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts of government agencies to establish PPE
balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and
accountability.

6.9 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE), strictly:
a) Mandate the Accountant and GSO to promptly and
meticulously post the acquisition of PPE to the respective
ledger cards and property cards upon receipt of the source
documents and keep and maintain the same in accordance
with the provisions of the Manual;
b) Mandate the GSO to retain and keep copies of the AIR,
PAR, and ARE as prescribed; and
c) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual
detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and
submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than
January 31 of succeeding year.

7.6 Hence, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly
direct the concerned personnel to:
a. Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-
repair inspection and post repair inspection to LGU
properties that are subjected to repair and furnish the
reports to the COA Auditors within five (5) days from the
date of each of the inspection activity;
b. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain the records
regarding the history of repairs of each of the properties of
the LGU; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims
pertaining to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and
heavy equipment with lacking supporting documents.

8.7 We recommended the management to submit the 20%
Development Fund on the structural projects for contract review
and inspection, in accordance with Section 122 of the Presidential
Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government
Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.1.4 to 3.1.7 of the
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 12, 2009.

9.5 We recommended that all accountable officers prepare and submit
the RAAF to the Office of the Treasurer at the end of each month
to ensure all accountable forms issued are properly recorded and
reported. Also, we recommend that the Office of the Municipal
Treasurer prepare and submit the CRAAF to the Office of the
Auditor as required under Section 51 of the NGAS, Volume II, for
LGUs.

10.6 In order to ensure the timely submission of required report and
disbursement vouchers (DVs), we recommended to the LCE to
instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
submit the required reports and DVs within the prescribed time
given by this Commission pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and
other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

11.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) should
(a) instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General
Services Officer to reconcile their books and recalculate the amount
of both depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation, (b)
reflect and present the Property, Plant and Equipment Account
(PPE) in the FS with correct amounts, and (c) instruct the General
Services Officer to submit to the audit team the Report of the
Physical Count on the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE),
pursuant to Section 262 of the Government Accounting Manual
(GAM) for Local Government Units, Volume I and Section 124 (2)
of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting System (PPSAS) 17 –
Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

12.7 As required by COA Circular 2015-008, we recommended the
Honorable Mayor to:
a. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to
conduct the physical count of its Road Network and
prepare a report thereon;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the Local
Road Network account based on the result of the physical
count and disclose the said account in the Notes to
Financial Statements in compliance with Item VI.2 of
COA 2015-008;
c. Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain a
Local Road Inventory and Road Map and prepare a
report thereon at the end of every accounting period as
well as keep a complete Local Road Network Property
Card for all roads and its components; and
d. Require Municipal Engineer to provide the municipal
accountant and the GSO with complete description and
cost of segregation of road components for road projects.

13.20 We recommended the management to:
a. Implement the perpetual inventory method in recording
purchases and movements of the supplies, in accordance
with Sections 182 to 190, Government Accounting Manual
for LGUs, Volume I;
b. The management shall recognize Supplies Inventory at its
book first and follow the moving average method for costing
in accordance with IPSAS 12, par 35 and 44 and sections 180
to 181, respectively; and
c. Conduct physical count at the year-end for faithful
compliance with Section 194 of GAM for LGUs, Volume 1.

14.6 We required the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO)
to immediately furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of
Acceptance & Inspection Report within 24 hours after acceptance
of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and
acceptance by the LGU personnel concerned on other items as
required by Section 114 (d) & 116 of COA No.92-386 date October
12, 1992 and Sec. 5.01.04 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May
18, 1995 so that timely inspection of deliveries can be made by the
audit team.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the municipal General Services Officer to abide with the GAM for LGUs and to submit the breakdown on the other Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses.
observations.managementAction: The management focused on the said matter.

observations.recommendation: In compliance with the GAM for LGUs, we recommended to the Honorable Mayor to: to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the reports for special requirements as required by Chapter 5, Sections 143 to 144, 159 and 161 to 163 of GAM for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Implemente d

observations.recommendation: In order to ensure the timely submission of required report and disbursement vouchers (DVs), we recommended to the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required reports and DVs within the prescribed time given by the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) should (a) instruct
the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to
reconcile their books and recalculate the amount of both depreciation expense
and accumulated depreciation, (b) reflect and present the Property, Plant and
Equipment Account (PPE) in the FS with correct amounts, and (c) instruct the
General Services Officer to submit to the audit team the Report of the Physical
Count on the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), pursuant to Section
262 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government
Units, Volume I and Section 124 (2) of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting
System (PPSAS) 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

As required by COA Circular 2015-008, we recommend the Honorable Mayor
to:
a. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the physical count of
its Road Network and prepare a report thereon;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the Local Road Network account
based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said account in the Notes
to Financial Statements in compliance with Item VI.2 of COA 2015-008;
c. Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain a Local Road Inventory and
Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every accounting period as
well as keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its
components; and
d. Require Municipal Engineer to provide the municipal accountant and the GSO
with complete description and cost of segregation of road components for road
projects.

We recommended the management to:
a. Implement the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases and
movements of the supplies, in accordance with Sections 182 to 190,
Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Volume I;
b. The management shall recognize Supplies Inventory at its book first and
follow the moving average method for costing in accordance with IPSAS
12, par 35 and 44 and sections 180 to 181, respectively; and
c. Conduct physical count at the year-end for faithful compliance with
Section 194 of GAM for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to abide with Sections 61 and 111 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No.
1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and to
submit the breakdown on Other Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses.

We required the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to immediately
furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of Acceptance & Inspection Report within 24
hours after acceptance of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection
and acceptance by the LGU personnel concerned on other items as required by
Sections 114 (d) & 116 of COA No.92-386 dated October 12, 1992 and Sec. 5.01.04 of
COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995, so that timely inspection of deliveries
can be made by the audit team.

In compliance with the GAM for LGUs, we recommended to the Honorable
Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the
requirements of Chapter 5, Sections 143 to 144, 159 and 161 to 163 of Government
Accounting Manual for Local Government Units, Volume I.

In order to ensure the timely submission of required report and disbursement
vouchers (DVs), we recommended to the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required reports and DVs within the
prescribed time given by this Commission pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and
other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the management:
a. Implement the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases and movements of the supplies in accordance with Sec. 114, and Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS; b. The management should recognize Office Supplies Inventory at its book first and follow the moving average method for costing in accordance with IPSAS 12, par 44 and sec 115, respectively; and c. Conduct physical count at the year-end for faithful compliance with Sec. 124 of NGAS Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: hired GSO

observations.recommendation:
In adherence to the provisions of Section 20 of NGAS Volume I, we recommended that:
a. Require the municipal treasurer to furnish a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the municipal accountant. Based on the list, the municipal accountant shall prepare a JEV for the setting up of the real property tax receivable/special education tax receivable at the beginning of the year; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to make the appropriate JEV to set up the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivable using the following entries, supported with the supporting documents from the municipal treasurer.
Dr. Receivable account xx
Cr. Deferred Income xx
observations.managementAction: The accounting has reconciled with the treasury.

observations.recommendation:
For faithful compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002, we recommended that:
a. The Management shall open a separate special trust fund account; and
b. The unexpended LDRRMF for the year end shall accrue to a special trust fund solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC within the next five (5) years.
observations.managementAction: An account has been opened.

observations.recommendation: In order to ensure the utilization of the required 20% Development Fund on purposes for which the fund is intended, it is recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and all officials responsible in the preparation of the budget and utilization of funds shall observe the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated September 20, 2005 and we are requiring the Municipal Accountant to submit DVs of such allocation.
observations.managementAction: The management complied and reconciled with the accounting.

observations.recommendation:
For the above deficiencies, we reiterated our previous audit
recommendations that the management shall:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct physical
count of PPE and submit the required inventory report on time for
faithful compliance with Sec. 124 of NGAS Volume 1; and
b. Require the designated Property/ Supply Office and the Municipal
Accountant to maintain their respective property ledger cards for
reconciliation and control purposes, and submit the Inventory Report
per requirements of Sec 114 of NGAS Volume I, Sec. 14 & 45 of NGAS
Volume II and Sec 490 of GAAM. As reconciled, for the Audit Team’s
review and verification

We recommended that the management shall:
a. Implement the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases and
movements of the supplies in accordance with Sec. 114, Volume I of the
Manual on the NGAS;
b. The management should recognize Office Supplies Inventory at its book
first and follow the moving average method for costing in accordance
with IPSAS 12, par 44 and sec 115, respectively; and
c. Conduct physical count at the year-end for faithful compliance with Sec.
124 of NGAS Volume 1.

In adherence to the provisions of Section 20 of NGAS Volume I, we
recommended that:
a. Require the municipal treasurer to furnish a duly certified list showing the
names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the
municipal accountant. Based on the list, the municipal accountant shall
prepare a JEV for the setting up of the real property tax receivable/special
education tax receivable at the beginning of the year; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to make the appropriate JEV to set up
the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivable
using the following entries, supported with the supporting documents from
the municipal treasurer.

Dr. Receivable account xx
Cr. Deferred Income xx

For faithful compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002, we recommended
that:
a. The Management shall open a separate special trust fund account; and
b. The unexpended LDRRMF for the year end shall accrue to a special
trust fund solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction
and management activities of the LDRRMC within the next five (5) years.

In order to ensure the utilization of the required 20% Development Fund on
purposes for which the fund is intended, it is recommended that the Municipal
Budget Officer and all officials responsible in the preparation of the budget
and utilization of funds shall observe the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated September 20, 2005 and we are
requiring the Municipal Accountant to submit DVs of such allocation.

In order to ensure the timely submission of required report and DVs, we
recommended to the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer to submit the required reports and DVs within the prescribed time
given by the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445.

We recommend the Management to submit the approved Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and regularly the Accomplishment Report on the utilization of 20% EDF showing the category and details of the projects/activities financed under the funds.
observations.managementAction: Approved Annual Investment Plan

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team the abovementioned Checks for verification.
observations.managementAction: Cancelled checks submitted.

observations.recommendation: The Management should instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to finish the physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and coordinate with the Accounting office for reconciliation of the records.
observations.managementAction: The management has started identifying its PPEs; no designated GSO.
observations.reason: The physical count and reconciliation not made due to no designated GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC will prepare the prescribed Procurement Report (PMR) that would represent all procurement activities undertaken for each semester and status of project implementation and submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: PMR and status of project implementa tion were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to instruct the BAC to submit copies of perfected contracts, bidding documents and other supporting documents to the Auditor within the period prescribed in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to effect timely review of the perfected contracts and its supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the copy of perfected contracts and other supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to instruct the Planning Officer to submit a list of all on-going projects and those that are to be implemented during the year including the essential features of the projects to the Auditor within the period prescribed in COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: List of projects for the year 2018 was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to reflect the transactions accordingly to the RCIC.
observations.managementAction: Proper numbering of DVs implemented.

observations.recommendation:
a. Instruct the account to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for the period January to December 2016.
b. Request the Treasurer to retrieve all bank statements for the year 2016 and furnish the same to the Accountant for the preparation of the BRS.

We recommend that the concerned accountable municipal officials be instructed to submit immediately all the reports required from their Offices. The prompt and regular submission of these reports must always be given emphasis.
a. The Management should resubmit the following set of financial statements in complete substance and form.
a. Submit the DVs for the Trust Funds, financial statements and other reports being prepared by the Municipal Accountants and Municipal Treasurer.
